sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Brighton & Hove Albion are struggling, with no wins in their last 3 matches, managing only 2 draws and a loss, though they’ve scored in all of their last 5 games. Crystal Palace are in worse shape, winless in their last 12 matches, with a mix of draws and losses, and they’ve conceded in 4 of their last 5 games. Both teams are showing defensive vulnerabilities recently.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, results have been tight, with a 0-0 draw in November 2025 and a 2-1 Crystal Palace win in April 2025. Historically, matches between these sides often see both teams scoring (8 of last 10), and cards are frequent, with over 4.5 per game in their last 5 head-to-heads.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Brighton, sitting 13th with 31 points, have scored 34 goals this season (12th in the league) but struggle defensively with 32 conceded. Crystal Palace, 15th with 29 points, have a weaker attack (25 goals) but a slightly better defense (29 conceded). Brighton’s possession play (52.5%) and pressing stats give them a slight edge at home.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Brighton’s Danny Welbeck (8 goals) will be crucial up front, while Pascal Groß’s creativity (3 assists in last 5 head-to-heads) could unlock Palace’s defense. For Palace, Ismaïla Sarr (4 goals) and Daniel Muñoz (3 goals, 2 assists) are threats, but the absence of Jean-Philippe Mateta (8 goals) might hurt their attack.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (38% home win, 30% draw, 32% away win), Brighton hold a marginal advantage at the American Express Stadium. Palace’s long winless streak and Brighton’s scoring consistency suggest a narrow home win, though a draw remains likely given recent head-to-head trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the score remains level at 0-0 between Brighton & Hove Albion and Crystal Palace at the American Express Stadium. Brighton have shown more promise in attack, creating a big chance and registering a shot on target, while Crystal Palace have struggled to create clear opportunities. Possession has been slightly in favor of Brighton, and they’ve shown more intent in the final third with several entries into the penalty area. However, neither side has managed to convert their efforts into goals, setting up an intriguing second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Brighton's attacking intent
halftimeSections.text: Brighton have displayed a more threatening presence in the attacking areas compared to their opponents. They've created a notable opportunity but failed to capitalize on it, indicating a need for sharper finishing. Players like Kaoru Mitoma and Pascal Groß have been influential in creating chances, with key passes that have troubled the Palace defense. Brighton’s frequent entries into dangerous areas suggest they have the potential to unlock the game if they maintain this level of intensity.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Crystal Palace's defensive resilience
halftimeSections.text: Crystal Palace have managed to hold firm defensively despite Brighton’s pressure. Their backline, marshaled by players like Maxence Lacroix and Jefferson Lerma, has been effective in clearing danger and winning duels, particularly in the air. However, their lack of attacking threat is evident, with no significant chances created in the first half. Palace will need to find a way to transition more effectively if they are to turn the tide in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Brighton dominate the early stages with consistent spells of pressure in the opposition half, particularly in the opening minutes and around the midway point. They maintained a strong attacking presence for extended periods, pushing forward with intent. Crystal Palace, on the other hand, had a slow start but grew into the game towards the latter part of the half, showing increased momentum and sustaining pressure in the closing minutes. The shifts in momentum indicate both teams have had their moments, but Brighton’s earlier dominance could give them a psychological edge.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Brighton, Kaoru Mitoma has been a standout with his creativity and ability to unsettle defenders, making him a likely candidate to influence the game further. Lewis Dunk also poses a threat from set-pieces, given his involvement in attacking situations. On the Crystal Palace side, Maxence Lacroix has been a rock at the back, and his ability to win duels could be crucial in keeping Brighton at bay. Ismaila Sarr might be the key to unlocking Brighton’s defense if Palace can get him more involved in the attack.
